title: Debug missing or excessive recomposition
description: Diagnose a Compose UI that won't update (or updates too much) by checking state observability and read scope.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the data the composable should react to and how it's stored. 2. Confirm it's observable Compose state (mutableStateOf / collected StateFlow), not a plain var. 3. Verify the composable actually reads the state in its scope so a change invalidates it. 4. For excessive recomposition, check unstable parameters, reading state too high in the tree, and missing remember; use the recomposition counts/layout-inspector. 5. Hoist or stabilize state appropriately. Rollback point: before restructuring state hoisting (changes ownership). Validation: the UI updates on state change and recomposes only the affected scope; a plain var that never triggers recomposition, or whole-screen recomposition on every keystroke, are the two failure modes this targets.
